rationale: Portugal's main exposure this cycle is the World Bank's warning of a post-COVID economic low driven by US-Iran conflict, which would hit EU energy prices and tourism demand; all other headlines are distant or irrelevant.

top_events.title: World Bank: US-Iran war to pull global economy to post-COVID low
top_events.summary: A global downturn would suppress tourism revenues, raise energy costs, and strain Portugal's EU-dependent economy significantly.

top_events.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes
top_events.summary: El Niño conditions raise risk of drought and wildfires in Iberia, a recurring and serious threat to Portugal's agriculture and tourism.

top_events.title: Gold is now the top reserve asset — dollar dominance at risk
top_events.summary: Shift away from dollar dominance could affect EU financial stability and Atlantic trade dynamics, with modest indirect impact on Portugal.
